label: Systems Engineer
instruction: Emphasise systems-level thinking, integration across disciplines, requirements flow-down, and end-to-end product architecture.

label: Mechanical Engineer
instruction: Emphasise mechanical design, CAD, materials, mechanisms, and manufacturing-aware engineering.

label: Senior Mechanical Engineer
instruction: Emphasise senior mechanical ownership, technical judgment, mentoring, and delivery of complex mechanical workstreams.

label: Product Development Engineer
instruction: Emphasise product development lifecycle ownership from concept through validation and production transfer.

label: Biomedical Engineer
instruction: Emphasise biomedical engineering, clinical context, biocompatible materials, and patient-facing device constraints.

label: Medical Device Engineer
instruction: Emphasise medical-device development, device classifications, and regulated design controls.

label: Mechatronics Engineer
instruction: Emphasise mechatronics integration across mechanical, electrical, firmware, and software domains.

label: R&D Engineer
instruction: Emphasise research and development experimentation, prototyping, and novel technical problem solving.

label: Technical Lead
instruction: Emphasise technical leadership, design reviews, architecture decisions, and guiding engineering quality.

label: Product Lead
instruction: Emphasise product leadership, stakeholder management, prioritisation, and cross-functional delivery ownership.

label: Medical Devices
instruction: Highlight medical-device programmes, implant/instrument work, and MedTech product delivery.

label: Consumer Products
instruction: Highlight transferable product-development practices relevant to consumer hardware where supported by evidence.

label: Regulated Product Development
instruction: Emphasise regulated development, design controls, risk management, and compliance-aware engineering.

label: Design for Manufacture
instruction: Emphasise design for manufacture, process capability, tolerance strategy, and production readiness.

label: Verification and Validation
instruction: Emphasise verification and validation planning, protocols, evidence generation, and test readiness.

label: Systems Engineering
instruction: Emphasise systems engineering methods, interfaces, requirements traceability, and integration.

label: Mechanical Design
instruction: Emphasise detailed mechanical design quality, CAD fluency, and mechanism/component design outcomes.

label: Technical Leadership
instruction: Emphasise mentoring, review leadership, and coordinating technical contributors without inventing unsupported claims.
